title: Houses for Rent in Puritas, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Puritas, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in Puritas, Cleveland: rent ranges, transit access, and nearby areas from a local property manager. Tell us what you're looking for.
answerBox: Puritas is part of the Bellaire-Puritas area on Cleveland's far west side, near I-71 and Cleveland Hopkins International Airport. Citywide, houses typically rent from about $1,100 to $1,350 a month depending on size (Zumper, July 2026), and every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 vouchers.

body: Puritas is part of the **Bellaire-Puritas** Statistical Planning Area on Cleveland's far west side, close to Interstate 71 and the Cleveland Hopkins International Airport corridor. It's a primarily residential area bordering the **West Park** section of the city, which includes the Jefferson and Kamm's Corners neighborhoods.

We don't currently have a concentrated portfolio of rental homes in the Puritas area — our Section 8-friendly inventory is centered on Cleveland's East and Southeast sides, with a smaller presence on the near-west side. If you're set on this part of the city, [tell our leasing team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll let you know if something nearby fits, or browse our [Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to see where we currently manage homes.

sections.h2: How much do houses rent for near Puritas?
body: Cleveland-wide rents vary a lot by bedroom count and building age, and neighborhood-level data for a smaller area like Puritas isn't separately published. The table below uses citywide figures from Zumper's July 2026 rent report — a reasonable starting point for budgeting anywhere in the city, including the far west side.

For comparison, Cleveland overall runs about 36% below the $1,950 national median rent (Zumper, July 2026), so even on the higher end, Cleveland rentals tend to be more affordable than many other U.S. metro areas.

Puritas sits near Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, which is also the western terminus of the **RTA Red Line** — the region's heavy-rail line running from the airport through downtown Tower City to Cleveland's East Side. Interstate 71 runs along the area's eastern edge, giving quick highway access toward downtown and the southern suburbs.
As with any address, confirm the closest bus routes and rail stop directly with [Greater Cleveland RTA](https://www.riderta.com/routes) before choosing a home.

sections.h2: Nearby neighborhoods on the far west side
body: Puritas neighbors the **West Park** section of Cleveland, which includes the Jefferson and Kamm's Corners areas — both, like Puritas, sit on the city's far west side near the airport corridor. If you want to compare options, see our guides to [houses for rent in Kamm's Corners](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-kamm-s-corners-cleveland/) and [houses for rent in Old Brooklyn](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/), a nearby southwest-side neighborhood where we do manage Section 8-friendly homes.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers if you're renting on the west side
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept housing vouchers, and the City of Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance — those protections exist only in a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs, such as Cleveland Heights and South Euclid, as of July 2026. That means voucher acceptance varies by landlord and by area, including on the far west side.

Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how CMHA's voucher program works and where our current inventory is concentrated.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses in Puritas?
faq.a: Our current portfolio is concentrated on Cleveland's East and Southeast sides, with a smaller presence on the near-west side. We don't have a regular inventory in the Puritas area specifically, but availability changes, so it's worth telling our team what you're looking for — we may know of a nearby option or can point you toward areas where we do manage Section 8-friendly homes.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Cleveland near Puritas?
faq.a: Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts median rent around $1,250 a month, with two-bedroom homes averaging about $1,100 and three-bedroom homes around $1,350. Neighborhood-specific data for Puritas isn't separately published, so these citywide figures are the best available starting point for budgeting.

faq.q: What is the closest RTA line to Puritas?
faq.a: Puritas is near Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, the western terminus of the RTA Red Line, which runs through downtown Tower City to Cleveland's East Side. Bus routes also serve the far west side; check current routes and schedules directly with Greater Cleveland RTA before signing a lease.

faq.q: Does Cleveland protect Section 8 voucher holders from discrimination?
faq.a: Not currently. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, and the City of Cleveland has not enacted a voucher-protection ordinance as of July 2026, though several suburbs — including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, and University Heights — have local protections. Always confirm a landlord's voucher policy directly before applying.

faq.q: Where does Rent Finder Cleveland manage rental homes?
faq.a: We manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, concentrated in East and Southeast Cleveland neighborhoods such as Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, and Buckeye-Shaker, with additional homes on the west side and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio. Every home accepts Section 8 vouchers. Book a showing to see current availability.
